title: Exploring the API with kubectl explain
description:
## Exploring the API with `kubectl explain`

`kubectl explain` is a built-in reference tool that describes the fields of any Kubernetes resource — directly from the live API server. It is invaluable during exams when you need to recall the exact field name or understand what a field accepts.

**Your task:**

A ConfigMap has been created for you. Use `kubectl explain` to answer the following, then:

1. Create a Pod named `explain-demo` using image `nginx:alpine`
2. Set the pod's `spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds` to **5** (use `kubectl explain pod.spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds` to understand the field)

```bash
# Explore the pod spec:
kubectl explain pod.spec

# Inspect a specific field:
kubectl explain pod.spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds
```

hints.title: kubectl explain syntax
hints.body: Use dot-notation to drill into nested fields. For example: `kubectl explain pod.spec.containers.resources`.

hints.title: Create the pod with the field set
hints.body: Use a heredoc to pipe a YAML manifest with terminationGracePeriodSeconds set to 5 directly to kubectl apply.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: explain-demo
spec:
  terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 5
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:alpine
EOF
